title: Create meeting intelligence packet from operational notes
given: A demo operator has CRM, Control Tower, or supplier view access.
The tenant has assistant email threads, CRM activities, shipment notes/exceptions, and supplier onboarding notes.
Some notes include actions, risks, decisions, and sensitive contact details.
when: The operator selects Create meeting packet.
then: The assistant persists an AssistantMeetingIntelligencePacket row.
The packet includes transcript digests, redactions, extracted actions, risks, decisions, object links, and draft minutes.
No CRM activity, shipment note, supplier task, email reply, or action queue item is created or changed automatically.

title: Queue extracted meeting work for human review
given: A meeting intelligence packet exists with extracted follow-up actions and risks.
The operator has edit access to at least one meeting intelligence source domain.
when: The operator adds a review note.
then: An AssistantActionQueueItem is created for meeting intelligence review.
The packet status changes to REVIEW_QUEUED and stores the approval note.
The audit event states that source records and follow-up tasks were not created or mutated automatically.

title: Redacted minutes with linked operational objects
given: A transcript-style source contains an email address or phone number.
The source is linked to a CRM account, shipment, or supplier.
when: The assistant builds the live meeting preview.
The operator reviews the extracted minutes.
then: Sensitive values are replaced with redaction markers in digests and minutes evidence.
Object links point back to the CRM, Control Tower, or supplier records allowed by permissions.
Draft minutes clearly remain review-only until approved.
